metadata.title: MB to KB converter – convert MB to KB
metadata.description: Convert megabytes to kilobytes and back. 1 MB is 1,024 KB in the binary system. Type a value and the result appears instantly. Runs in your browser with no sign-up.

hero.title: Convert megabytes to kilobytes
hero.description: Type a number of megabytes and read the result in kilobytes straight away. A megabyte is 1,024 kilobytes in the binary system, so the converter multiplies your value by 1,024. Use Swap order to convert kilobytes to megabytes instead.

software.name: Megabytes to kilobytes converter – MB to KB calculator
alternateName: How many KB in a MB
Which is bigger MB or KB
software.description: Megabytes to kilobytes converter that runs in your browser with no sign-up. Type a number of megabytes and read the result in kilobytes instantly. It uses the binary system, where 1 MB is 1,024 KB.
featureList: Converts megabytes to kilobytes and kilobytes to megabytes in the binary system (1 MB = 1,024 KB)
The conversion works both ways — the Swap order button switches the direction
The result appears instantly as you type, with no button to click
The Copy result button puts the finished value on your clipboard
Accepts both a dot and a comma as the decimal separator
Standard toggle: compute in binary (1,024, like Windows and RAM) or decimal (1,000, like drives, macOS and search engines)
Gives a ready-made formula for Excel and spreadsheets
Every calculation runs locally in your browser, so your data never leaves your device
The exact formula is: kilobytes = megabytes × 1,024
Works on phone, tablet and computer with no installation

howTo.name: How do you convert megabytes to kilobytes?
howTo.description: A short guide to using the megabytes to kilobytes converter.

steps.name: Type a number of megabytes
steps.text: In the left field, type the number of megabytes you want to convert to kilobytes. You can use a dot or a comma as the decimal separator.

steps.name: Read the result in kilobytes
steps.text: The result in kilobytes appears immediately in the field on the right. The Copy result button puts it on your clipboard.

steps.name: Swap the direction if you need to
steps.text: Click Swap order to move to converting kilobytes to megabytes.

contentBlocks.title: Why convert megabytes to kilobytes?
contentBlocks.html:
A megabyte is larger than a kilobyte: 1 MB is 1,024 KB. File sizes are usually given in megabytes, but some systems, forms and limits expect a value in kilobytes — that is when you convert MB to KB.

The relationship is fixed: 1 MB is 1,024 KB in the binary system, which operating systems and this converter use. To turn megabytes into kilobytes, multiply their number by 1,024. The converter does this the moment you type a value.

Watch out for two systems. In the decimal system (SI), 1 MB is 1,000 KB — that is how storage makers count, for example. This converter uses the binary system (1,024 KB), matching what Windows and macOS show.

This tool computes both standards: binary (1,024) by default, and the Unit standard button switches it to decimal (1,000). Windows and RAM count in binary, while drive makers, macOS and search engines count in decimal — which is why the same capacity is quoted two different ways.
contentBlocks.imageAlt: Free megabytes to kilobytes converter – Arteon

contentBlocks.title: How does the megabytes to kilobytes conversion work? Formula and example
contentBlocks.html:
The conversion rests on a fixed relationship: one megabyte is 1,024 kilobytes. To turn megabytes into kilobytes, multiply their number by 1,024.

Full formula: kilobytes = megabytes × 1,024. The other way: megabytes = kilobytes ÷ 1,024.

Example: a file is 5 MB. Calculation: 5 × 1,024 = 5,120 KB. Likewise 25 MB is 25,600 KB, because each megabyte is made up of 1,024 kilobytes.

Which is bigger? The megabyte. 1 MB is 1,024 KB, so a 2 MB file is over two thousand times bigger than a 1 KB file. This is a common source of confusion when comparing sizes given in different units.

In Excel, type the formula =A1*1024 in a cell, where A1 holds the value in MB. The other way, kilobytes to megabytes, use =A1/1024.

contentBlocks.title: Megabytes to kilobytes conversion table
contentBlocks.description: The table below converts common megabyte values into kilobytes in the binary system (×1,024). To convert another value, use the converter above.
contentBlocks.caption: Example: 1 MB × 1,024 = 1,024 KB. Want to convert kilobytes to megabytes? Click Swap order in the converter above.

contentBlocks.title: The full data unit ladder: from smallest to largest
contentBlocks.description: Every data size unit grows by the same multiplier: each one is 1,024 times larger than the previous. The table below shows the order from byte to petabyte, with a typical example of what each unit describes.

rows.label: Megabyte (MB)
values: a photo or an MP3 song

rows.label: Gigabyte (GB)
values: a movie or a RAM stick

contentBlocks.caption: Order from smallest to largest: byte, kilobyte, megabyte, gigabyte, terabyte, petabyte. Each step is a multiplication by 1,024.

contentBlocks.title: When do people most often convert megabytes to kilobytes?
contentBlocks.description: You convert sizes in megabytes to kilobytes mostly for limits and comparisons.

items.title: Limits given in KB
items.description: Some CMS platforms, upload forms and config files state a size limit in kilobytes. When you know the size in megabytes, you convert it to KB, for example 2 MB is 2,048 KB.

items.title: Comparing with small files
items.description: Icons, CSS and JSON files are counted in kilobytes, while photos and documents are in megabytes. Bringing them to kilobytes makes them easy to compare, for example 1 MB is 1,024 KB next to a 50 KB file.

items.title: An exact size in KB
items.description: Sometimes an asset spec calls for a value in kilobytes. From a size of 10 MB you get exactly 10,240 KB, with no manual multiplication.

items.title: Which is bigger: MB or KB
items.description: The converter shows plainly that the megabyte is bigger: 1 MB is 1,024 KB. This is the most common question when sizes are given in different units.

contentBlocks.title: Frequently asked questions about converting megabytes to kilobytes

items.question: How many KB are in 1 MB?
items.answer: 1 MB is 1,024 KB in the binary system, which this converter and operating systems use. In the decimal system (SI), used by storage makers among others, 1 MB is 1,000 KB.

items.question: Which is bigger, MB or KB?
items.answer: The megabyte. 1 MB is 1,024 KB, so a megabyte is over a thousand times larger than a kilobyte. A 2 MB file is bigger than a 500 KB file, even though the number next to KB looks larger.

items.question: How much is 1 MB in KB?
items.answer: 1 MB is exactly 1,024 KB. Calculation: 1 × 1,024 = 1,024 KB. This is the base relationship between the megabyte and the kilobyte, which is why it is looked up most often.

items.question: How do you convert megabytes to kilobytes?
items.answer: Multiply the number of megabytes by 1,024. For example, 50 MB × 1,024 = 51,200 KB. The converter does this the moment you type a value, with no button to click.

items.question: How many KB are 5, 25 and 100 MB?
items.answer: 5 MB is 5,120 KB, 25 MB is 25,600 KB, and 100 MB is 102,400 KB. You get each of these by multiplying the number of megabytes by 1,024.

items.question: How do you convert kilobytes to megabytes?
items.answer: Divide the number of kilobytes by 1,024. For example, 5,120 KB ÷ 1,024 = 5 MB. Click Swap order to have the converter work in this direction.

items.question: Is 1 MB 1,000 or 1,024 KB?
items.answer: It depends on the system. Operating systems and this converter use the binary system, where 1 MB is 1,024 KB. Storage makers use the decimal system, where 1 MB is 1,000 KB. That is why the same number of megabytes can give a slightly different result in kilobytes.

items.question: How do you convert MB to KB in Excel?
items.answer: Type the formula =A1*1024 in a cell, where A1 holds the value in megabytes. The other way, kilobytes to megabytes, use =A1/1024. The same formula works in Google Sheets.

items.question: What is the order of the units from smallest to largest?
items.answer: From smallest: byte (B), kilobyte (KB), megabyte (MB), gigabyte (GB), terabyte (TB), petabyte (PB). Each unit is 1,024 times larger than the previous one. A byte describes a single character, while a terabyte describes a drive capacity.

contentBlocks.title: Have a question or suggestion?
contentBlocks.description: If you have spotted an error in the table or have an idea to improve the converter, write to us. We reply to every message.
